prompt:
Question: Which of the following passages is the original verbatim passage? The other passages are paraphrased versions of it.

Options:
A. Since May–June 2022, a series of labour strikes and industrial disputes have occurred in various industries of the United Kingdom's economy as workers walked out over pay and conditions. The strikes took place with rising inflation, and demands for pay increases that would keep pace with this inflation. The strikes began on 15 June 2022 after members of the National Union of Rail, Maritime and Transport Workers (RMT) voted to strike over planned changes to their pay and working conditions. They were soon joined by other railway unions, and a series of one-day strikes halted trains in many parts of the British mainland. Train services operated at 20% of normal capacity on strike days. Public support for the strikes was organised through the Enough is Enough! campaign. With
B. Beginning in May–June 2022, the economy of the United Kingdom experienced a sequence of labor strikes and industrial disagreements across multiple sectors, as employees initiated walkouts to protest issues concerning their compensation and terms of employment. These industrial actions unfolded amidst a period of escalating inflation and were driven by calls for salary increases that would match the rate of this inflation. The initial strikes commenced on June 15, 2022, following a vote by members of the National Union of Rail, Maritime and Transport Workers (RMT) to take industrial action in response to proposed modifications to their wages and working arrangements. Subsequently, other railway unions joined these efforts, leading to a succession of one-day strikes that brought train operations to a standstill in numerous regions of the British mainland. On the days these strikes occurred, train services were operational at only 20% of their customary capacity. Public backing for these strikes was coordinated through the "Enough is Enough!" initiative.
C. Since May-June 2022, a series of industrial disputes and labor strikes have occurred in various sectors of the UK's economy. These strikes were caused by rising inflation and calls for faster pay increases that would keep pace with inflation. The strikes began on June 15, 2022, when the National Union of Rail, Maritime and Transport Workers (RMT) voted to strike over proposed changes to pay and working conditions. Other railway unions soon joined the strikes, and a series of one-day strikes brought trains to a halt in many parts of the UK.

Answer:

options.A: Since May–June 2022, a series of labour strikes and industrial disputes have occurred in various industries of the United Kingdom's economy as workers walked out over pay and conditions. The strikes took place with rising inflation, and demands for pay increases that would keep pace with this inflation. The strikes began on 15 June 2022 after members of the National Union of Rail, Maritime and Transport Workers (RMT) voted to strike over planned changes to their pay and working conditions. They were soon joined by other railway unions, and a series of one-day strikes halted trains in many parts of the British mainland. Train services operated at 20% of normal capacity on strike days. Public support for the strikes was organised through the Enough is Enough! campaign. With
options.B: Beginning in May–June 2022, the economy of the United Kingdom experienced a sequence of labor strikes and industrial disagreements across multiple sectors, as employees initiated walkouts to protest issues concerning their compensation and terms of employment. These industrial actions unfolded amidst a period of escalating inflation and were driven by calls for salary increases that would match the rate of this inflation. The initial strikes commenced on June 15, 2022, following a vote by members of the National Union of Rail, Maritime and Transport Workers (RMT) to take industrial action in response to proposed modifications to their wages and working arrangements. Subsequently, other railway unions joined these efforts, leading to a succession of one-day strikes that brought train operations to a standstill in numerous regions of the British mainland. On the days these strikes occurred, train services were operational at only 20% of their customary capacity. Public backing for these strikes was coordinated through the "Enough is Enough!" initiative.
options.C: Since May-June 2022, a series of industrial disputes and labor strikes have occurred in various sectors of the UK's economy. These strikes were caused by rising inflation and calls for faster pay increases that would keep pace with inflation. The strikes began on June 15, 2022, when the National Union of Rail, Maritime and Transport Workers (RMT) voted to strike over proposed changes to pay and working conditions. Other railway unions soon joined the strikes, and a series of one-day strikes brought trains to a halt in many parts of the UK.
